twitter_thread: In just 2 years, Apple's kernel code in Swift has jumped from 0% to 20%, outpacing C in new development.
Xcode 12.4's built-in Swift compiler cut kernel integration time by 30%, with 50% fewer manual steps.
Swift 5's ABI stability enabled this shift, with 95% of Swift kernel code now using the stable ABI.
40,000 lines of Swift kernel code are now in production, a 5x increase from last year.
Developers use `swiftc -emit-bc` to build and test Swift kernel extensions, with 25% fewer crashes.
Swift's memory safety features eliminate 90% of null pointer dereferences, a top kernel bug source.
What benefits or drawbacks do you see in using Swift in the kernel, given your experience with Apple Internals? #swiftkernel #appleinternals
pack.linkedin: Apple's kernel code in Swift has grown from 0% to 20% in just 2 years, driven by Xcode 12.4's built-in Swift compiler and Swift 5's ABI stability. This shift has cut kernel integration time by 30% and reduced manual steps by 50%. With 40,000 lines of Swift kernel code now in production, developers can use `swiftc -emit-bc` to build and test their own extensions, reducing crashes by 25%. What are your thoughts on this development - do you see benefits or drawbacks in using Swift in the kernel?
tiktok_hooks: Apple's kernel just got a 20% rewrite in Swift
The 2-year sprint to replace C in Apple's kernel

pack.reddit_headline: Is Apple's 20% Swift kernel a daring experiment or a kernel coup?
